the right is Vivasvat; next to him is
Indra; then going up to the west of Brahman there are Mitra (in 3
divisions) and Rajayaksman; then turning to the east, there are
Prthvldhara (in 3) and Apavatsa. In this manner these eight deities
are situated on the circumference around Brahman. In the internal
north-eastern corner (behind Parjanya), there is Apah; in the south-
eastern corner (between Savitr and Anila), SavItraJ in the south-
western corner, Jaya; and in the north-western corner, Rudra.

One group consisting of five deities that own one division each
viz. Apah, Apavatsa, Parjanya, Agni (^ikhin) and Diti, is situated
in the north-eastern corner. In the same manner there are five
deities in each of the remaining three corners viz., south-east, south-
west and north-west. The remaining deities in the outer compart-
ments, 20 in number, own two squares each. The remaining 4
deities headed by Aryaman preside over three squares each (begin-
